commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0bf8a93ffecdb22907764c3a6539fb00517cf20e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds outcome-aware QMD memory search, threads it through fallback managers, treats internal subagent session keys as direct QMD scope, updates SDK exports/budget, and adds regression tests.

PR surface: Source +71, Tests +184, Other 0. Total +255 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection. Current main and `v2026.6.10` still force-sync after any empty memory_search result while QMD returns `[]` for scope-denied, empty-query, and no-collection paths; I did not run a live QMD repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR needs a contributor compile fix plus maintainer judgment on the public SDK outcome method, not a separate automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(memory): distinguish QMD search outcomes
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #96796.

This PR separates QMD search outcomes from plain zero-hit results so `memory_search` no longer treats every empty QMD result as a stale index signal. It also carries the follow-up fixes found during local runtime activation: internal subagent session keys now pass QMD direct-chat scope, and the fallback memory manager preserves `searchWithOutcome()` instead of dropping back to the legacy `search()`-only path.

## What Problem This Solves

Previously, `QmdMemoryManager.search()` returned `[]` for multiple distinct states, including policy/guard outcomes such as scope denial. The `memory_search` caller could not distinguish those states and could force `sync({ reason: "search", force: true })`, causing slow interactive searches even when the index was already clean or the search was denied.

During local activation, two adjacent runtime faults were confirmed:

- Subagent session keys such as `agent:vision:subagent:*` were normalized to `subagent:*`, then rejected by direct-chat QMD scope rules.
- A fallback wrapper could omit `searchWithOutcome()`, allowing callers to fall back to the old ambiguous zero-result behavior after QMD fallback.

## What Changed

- Adds shared memory search outcome types:
  - `MemorySearchOptions`
  - `MemorySearchOutcome`
  - `MemorySearchOutcomeResult`
- Adds optional `MemorySearchManager.searchWithOutcome()`.
- Keeps the existing `search(): Promise<MemorySearchResult[]>` contract compatible.
- Implements `QmdMemoryManager.searchWithOutcome()` with discriminated outcomes:
  - `ok`
  - `scope-denied`
  - `empty-query`
  - `no-collections`
- Updates `memory_search` to use detailed outcomes when available.
- Preserves legacy fallback behavior for managers without `searchWithOutcome()`.
- Gates forced sync after zero hits to detailed QMD searches where:
  - `outcome === "ok"`
  - `hits.length === 0`
  - `status().dirty === true`
- Treats internal `agent:<id>:subagent:<uuid>` session keys as direct QMD scope instead of unknown/denied scope.
- Threads `searchWithOutcome()` through `FallbackMemoryManager`, including builtin fallback wrapping for managers that only implement `search()`.

## Evidence

Initial PR validation:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
```

Result:

- 2 test files passed
- 159 tests passed

```bash
pnpm test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
```

Result:

- extension package boundary check passed
- 116 plugins compiled

Additional local validation after follow-up fixes:

```bash
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-scope.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-scope.test.ts
```

Result:

- 0 warnings
- 0 errors

```bash
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-scope.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
```

Result in this local clone:

- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-scope.test.ts`: 7 passed
- memory-core suites did not execute because this clone is missing `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` during test import, before the affected tests run. CI is expected to provide the full workspace dependency graph.

Runtime activation evidence from the downstream hotfix environment:

- `memory_search` passes for `corpus=memory`, `corpus=all`, and `corpus=sessions`.
- QMD main is clean (`dirty=no`), with 7845 chunks and 24 collections.
- No active `qmd embed` or `qmd sync` processes.
- No recent `ensureOpen`, `Cannot read properties`, `qmd search denied by scope`, or `reason=search` logs after the hotfix restart.
